task: Task #12 — verify Bug A fix path is viable server-side

migration.evidence: Old binary (PID 2569821) emitted 42 'relation recovery_failures does not exist' errors up to 2026-04-18T21:05:52Z UTC. After restart at 2026-04-18T21:20:31Z UTC (PID 2579943 = new binary), zero schema-missing errors; handler succeeds via missing_authenticator short-circuit path. Schema state not directly queryable (prod DB query blocked by sandbox).
migration.note: Direct _sqlx_migrations row read was blocked by sandbox permission (prod DB); inferred applied because (a) deploy.sh commit 6e697a4 runs SQL migrations before restart, (b) new handler queries run without table-missing errors post-restart.

verdict.rationale: Post-restart (2026-04-18T21:20Z) the new A7 handler is live and gracefully processing all reportRecoveryFailure calls that clients send without epoch authenticators. The 11-day outage ended at 21:20Z today. 110 handler invocations in last 6h, 0 schema errors post-restart. Schema state inferred from runtime success because direct DB query blocked.
verdict.gap: No direct DB evidence (pg_tables / _sqlx_migrations) captured — team-lead should confirm via a direct psql read when convenient, but the log-based evidence is authoritative for 'is the fix working right now'.
verdict.next_step: Proceed with client-side work. Clients sending proper epoch authenticators (post-A7 client rollout) will flow through the vote/quorum pyramid rather than short-circuiting. Recommend one follow-up psql spot-check at team-lead's convenience: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reset_votes; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM epoch_authenticators; — expect 0 until clients start emitting authenticators.
